is_hidden.description: Indicates if the article is visible on the site

version.description: The version number that is currently published

is_category_hidden.description: Indicates if category is visible on the site

order.description: The position inside the parent category

is_draft.description: Indicates if the article is marked as a draft

exclude.description: Indicates if the article is excluded from search results on user website

is_category.description: If the value is True, then the object has to considered as a category, otherwise it is an article

attachment_ids.description: The IDs of the files attached to the article

is_deleted.description: If the value is True, then it indicates that the article has been deleted

is_folder_type_category.description: If the value is True, then it indicates that the category is a folder type category

updated_on_timestamp.description: The last updated timestamp of the article

is_private.description: If the value is True, then the article can only be accessed by logged in users in the knowledge base site

is_latest_version.description: If the value is True, then the article is the latest version

contributors.description: The IDs of users who have contributed to the article

is_shared_article.description: If the value is True, then it indicates that it is a shared article

lang_code.description: The language code of the article or category

category_unique_id.description: A unique identifier for the category which is a combination of category ID and the language code

unique_id.description: A unique identifier for the article which is a combination of article ID and the language code

deleted_by.description: The ID of the user who deleted the article

deleted_at.description: The timestamp when the article was deleted

is_git_hub_entity.description: If the value is True, then it indicates that the article is synced from GitHub

original_article_id.description: The original article ID. Applicable only for shared articles.

match_level.description: Indicates how well the attribute matched the search query. Can be: none, partial, full
Indicates how well the attribute matched the search query. Can be: none, partial, full

matched_words.description: List of words from the query that matched the object.

hits.description: The list of articles that matched the search query.

nb_hits.description: The number of hits (articles) matched by the query.

page.description: The position of the current page (zero-based).

nb_pages.description: The number of returned pages. Calculation is based on the total number of hits (nbHits) divided by the number of hits per page (hitsPerPage), rounded up to the nearest integer.

hits_per_page.description: Maximum number of hits (articles) per page.

processing_time_ms.description: The time the server took to process the request, in milliseconds. This doesn’t include network time.

query.description: The query used to search the articles.

extension_data.description: Extention data for customer API response

success.description: Status indication for customer API response

extension_data.description: This is the Extension data object

stack_trace.description: A technical trace showing where the error occurred within the system. Intended for backend debugging.

description.description: A clear message explaining what caused the error. This helps quickly understand what went wrong.

error_code.description: A short, predefined code that identifies the type of error. Useful for logging the error or raising a support request.

custom_data.description: Any structured metadata for the error object.

errors.description: Errors in the customer API response

extension_data.description: Extension Data for customer Api warning

description.description: A plain message that describes the warning and helps understand what should be reviewed.

warning_code.description: A short, predefined code that uniquely identifies the warning type.

warnings.description: Warnings in the customer API response

extension_data.description: Extension data for customer Api response information

description.description: A plain message offering helpful context about the response, such as confirmation of fallback logic or skipped operations.

information.description: Information passed by the customer API response

Example for 200 response.summary: 200 response in searches for a phrase inside project version

hits.title: Understanding Home page builder
hits.content:
The home page is the first page an end-user will see when they visit your knowledge base. The Home Page Builder lets you customize your home page by adding links, changing colors, including categories from your knowledge base, and more. 
 The home page builder comes with an intuitive interface to design a professional looking front-page to your knowledge base. You do not need any developer support to create a good looking home page. 
 If you do not want a home page and would like to direct the user directly to the real content, you can easily turn off the home page for your knowledge base. 
 
                
                This is a sample article, you can safely delete it.

content.value:
The home page is the first page an end-user will see when they visit your knowledge base. The Home Page Builder lets you customize your home page by adding links, changing colors, including categories from your knowledge base, and more. 
 The home page builder comes with an intuitive

extension_data.description: Extension data for customer API response

success.description: Indicates the status of the API response. A value of true signifies that the request was successfully processed, while false indicates a failure or error occurred.

extension_data.description: This is the Extension data object

stack_trace.description: A technical trace showing where the error occurred within the system. Intended for backend debugging.

description.description: A clear message explaining what caused the error. This helps quickly understand what went wrong.

error_code.description: A short, predefined code that identifies the type of error. Useful for logging the error or raising a support request.

custom_data.description: Any structured metadata for the error object.

errors.description: A list of errors encountered during the API request. Each error object provides details about the problem, including an error code and a message explaining the issue. This field is populated when the request fails or encounters issues.

extension_data.description: Extension Data for customer Api warning

description.description: A plain message that describes the warning and helps understand what should be reviewed.

warning_code.description: A short, predefined code that uniquely identifies the warning type.

warnings.description: A list of warnings generated during the API request. These are non-critical issues or recommendations that might affect the request but won't stop it from processing. Each warning object provides a message to inform the user of potential problems.

extension_data.description: Extension data for customer Api response information

description.description: A plain message offering helpful context about the response, such as confirmation of fallback logic or skipped operations.

information.description: Contains additional non-critical information relevant to the request or response. This field provides extra details that might assist in understanding the context of the API response but is not essential for processing.

Example for 400 response due to project.summary: 400 bad response due to invalid Project version Id

errors.description: The supplied project version id does not exist in your project

Example for 400 response due to Lanuage.summary: 400 bad response due to invalid Language Code
